name: CADIZ INC
summary: Cadiz Inc. is a Southern California water solutions provider owning approximately 46,000 acres of land in the Mojave Desert with 2.5 million acre-feet of permitted water supply and 1 million acre-feet of groundwater storage capacity. The company operates the Mojave Groundwater Bank, 220 miles of pipeline infrastructure, 43 miles of pipeline right-of-way, agricultural land, and water filtration technology (acquired from ATEC Systems) that removes contaminants including arsenic, nitrates, and Chromium 6 from groundwater.

products.name: Mojave Groundwater Bank
products.description: Permitted groundwater conservation and storage project supplying up to 50,000 AFY for 50 years to Southern California communities.

products.name: Northern Pipeline
products.description: 220-mile converted steel pipeline transporting water from Cadiz Ranch across Kern and San Bernardino Counties to California's Central Valley.

products.name: Southern Pipeline
products.description: 43-mile water conveyance pipeline system co-located within ARZC railroad right-of-way connecting Cadiz Ranch to the Colorado River Aqueduct.

products.name: ATEC Water Filtration Systems
products.description: Specialized filtration systems removing iron, manganese, arsenic, nitrates, Chromium 6 and other groundwater contaminants.

products.name: Agricultural Operations
products.description: Farming approximately 1,000 acres of grain/alfalfa crops and leasing 2,100 acres to Fenner Valley Farms LLC.

partnerships.description: 99-year lease to co-locate Southern Pipeline within ARZC's 43-mile active railroad right-of-way.
Credit agreement for up to $51 million unsecured loan convertible into majority interest in Mojave Groundwater Bank storage cash flows, contributed to MWI.
Lease of 2,100 acres at Cadiz Ranch for farming activities.
Lease agreement to develop a solar-powered green hydrogen production facility on up to 3,000 acres at Cadiz Ranch.
